_note: This is your preferences + scoring rubric. It is injected into every fit-scoring and CV-tailoring call to sharpen accuracy. base_cv.json stays your factual CV; this file defines what you WANT and how roles should be scored.
name: Manoj Ram Mopati
headline: Data Scientist with 3+ years' experience across retail, consumer goods, insurance and lending analytics; strong in Marketing Mix Modelling, trade promotion optimisation, RFM segmentation, machine learning, Azure, PySpark, SQL, Python and Power BI; MSc Data Science from Coventry University; Microsoft certified in Azure, Power BI and Fabric.
seniority: mid-level / associate individual-contributor with 3+ years of experience. Two CO-PRIMARY job categories, both wanted equally: (1) DATA SCIENCE — Data Scientist, Associate Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, AI Engineer, Applied Scientist; (2) DATA ANALYSIS — Data Analyst, Data Analytics, Analytics Engineer, Business Intelligence / BI Analyst, Insight Analyst, Reporting/MI Analyst. Both categories are searched for every company. Data Analyst roles are especially common in civil service / government / NHS and are strongly wanted there. NOT senior, lead, principal, manager, head-of or people-management roles unless the role is genuinely hands-on and explicitly suitable for 3+ years experience.
certifications: Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst Associate — PL-300
Microsoft Certified: Fabric Analytics Engineer Associate
target_sectors: Analytics / Data Consulting with direct employer only
right_to_work: UK Graduate visa EXPIRES IN <08-2028> - eligible to work in the UK now without employer sponsorship. Will need Skilled Worker visa sponsorship later for long-term employment.
work_mode: hybrid, remote, on-site, open to relocate within the UK
must_haves: direct employer, not a recruitment agency
UK-based role or Remote UK role
real data science, machine learning, AI, analytics engineering or applied analytics responsibilities
hands-on Python, SQL, ML, Azure, PySpark, Power BI, GenAI/RAG or production analytics work
employer must either sponsor Skilled Worker visas now or be realistic for future sponsorship before Graduate visa expiry
minimum salary around £30,000 GBP or above
deal_breakers: recruitment agencies or staffing agencies unless the end employer is clearly named and suitable
senior, lead, principal, manager, head-of or director roles requiring leadership beyond 3+ years experience
roles requiring active SC, DV or other security clearance that I do not currently hold
defence, military or highly security-cleared roles where clearance is mandatory from day one
pure software engineering roles with no data, ML, AI or analytics component
unpaid internships, commission-only roles or training schemes disguised as jobs
roles outside the UK unless fully remote and UK work eligibility is acceptable
clinical, medical, pharma or regulated healthcare roles that does not require data science or analytical skills
automotive, aerospace or mechanical engineering roles with no data science or analytics scope
preferences_notes: I want TWO co-primary categories equally: Data Science roles AND Data Analysis roles. Both are searched for every target company. Highest-priority roles are direct-employer UK Data Scientist / Machine Learning Engineer / AI Engineer roles AND Data Analyst / Analytics Engineer / BI Analyst roles using Python, SQL, PySpark, Azure, Databricks, Power BI/Tableau, machine learning, GenAI/RAG, MMM, pricing, forecasting, segmentation, risk/fraud analytics or production analytics. Data Analyst roles are especially wanted in civil service / government / NHS and other large public-sector employers, which open many data-analyst positions and often sponsor. Strong preference for retail, FMCG/CPG, banking, fintech, insurance, lending, product-tech, government/civil service, utilities and transport sectors. Prioritise roles where my Infosys experience in Marketing Mix Modelling, trade-promotion optimisation, RFM segmentation, insurance claims analytics, Azure ML workflows, Power BI dashboards and end-to-end ML/analytics projects is directly relevant. Do not reject insurance/health-insurance analytics roles, but avoid clinical/pharma roles needing medical domain credentials. Sponsorship matters long term, so favour employers with Skilled Worker sponsor capability or large UK employers likely to sponsor.
portfolio_project_highlights: SRM & Trade Promotion Optimisation + Marketing Mix Modelling: PySpark, Databricks, Python, R, log-log elasticity, baseline vs incremental decomposition, OR-Tools optimisation, ROI and scenario simulation.
Enterprise Health-Insurance ML Pipeline: PySpark medallion architecture, Bronze/Silver/Gold layers, scikit-learn, Docker, GitHub Actions, CI/CD and batch-scored ML outputs.
RFM Debt-Recovery Analytics: SQL, Python, RFM segmentation, 200K+ customers, risk tiering, Streamlit dashboard and BI rebuild guide.
Customer Churn Prediction: Random Forest, FastAPI, Docker, Azure Container Apps and GitHub Actions CI/CD.
RAG Analytics Assistant: GenAI assistant using retrieval-augmented generation for source-grounded business-report summaries.
NLP Sentiment Analysis: TF-IDF, SVM, Naive Bayes and Random Forest for customer review sentiment classification.
Potato Disease Classifier: TensorFlow CNN, FastAPI and Streamlit.
Loan Approval Prediction, Gemstone Price Predictor, House Price Predictor, Breast Cancer Predictor and Student Performance Predictor: end-to-end applied ML projects.
scoring_rubric: Judge BOTH categories as first-class: DATA SCIENCE (Data Scientist/ML/AI/Applied Scientist) and DATA ANALYSIS (Data Analyst/Analytics Engineer/BI/Insight/MI Analyst). Score 90-100 for an excellent match: direct employer, UK-based, sponsor-capable or likely sponsor, hands-on mid-level IC role in EITHER category, strong Python/SQL and relevant stack (Azure/PySpark/ML for DS; SQL/Python/Power BI/Tableau/dbt for DA), and clear relevance to MMM, pricing, forecasting, segmentation, risk/fraud analytics, GenAI/RAG, MLOps, BI/reporting or production analytics. Score 80-89 for strong matches with minor gaps (partial stack, sector mismatch) — this INCLUDES solid Data Analyst / BI / Analytics Engineer roles at direct employers, and Data Analyst roles in civil service / government / NHS / large enterprise (treat these as strong, NOT backup). Score 65-79 for decent roles where title and level fit but domain or tooling is weaker, e.g. more generic analyst roles with some SQL/Python. Score 50-64 for weak backups such as Excel-light reporting with minimal SQL, or junior roles far below level. Score under 50 for senior/lead/manager/head roles, agencies, vague reposts, clearance-required roles, pure-Excel manual reporting with no SQL/BI, unrelated software roles, unpaid roles, or jobs with no realistic future sponsorship. Set ghost_flag=true for vague reposts, duplicate listings, expired roles, no employer name, no salary/location clarity, or job descriptions with very generic wording and no real responsibilities.
